triples.subject: Metformin

step2_semantic_retrieval.query: How does Metformin reduce insulin resistance?

dense_results.title: Metformin and insulin resistance: a systematic clinical review
dense_results.text: Meta-analysis of 42 RCTs confirms that metformin reduces fasting insulin, HOMA-IR index, and HbA1c in patients with insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes mellitus.

dense_results.title: Mechanisms of metformin action on glucose metabolism and insulin resistance
dense_results.text: This review covers the pleiotropic mechanisms of metformin, including mitochondrial complex I inhibition, AMPK activation, and insulin sensitization at the level of hepatic and peripheral tissues.

dense_results.title: AMPK-mediated phosphorylation of insulin signaling nodes
dense_results.text: AMPK directly phosphorylates IRS1 and modulates PI3K-AKT signaling, providing a molecular link between cellular energy status and insulin sensitivity.

dense_results.title: EGFR signaling crosstalk with PI3K-AKT-mTOR axis
dense_results.text: Activated EGFR recruits the p85 subunit of PI3K to phosphorylated Y1068, generating PIP3 and triggering AKT phosphorylation at T308 and S473, with subsequent mTORC1 activation.

dense_results.title: Metformin activates AMP-activated protein kinase in hepatocytes
dense_results.text: Metformin treatment in primary hepatocytes led to significant AMPK phosphorylation and subsequent inhibition of mTORC1, reducing hepatic glucose production via suppression of the gluconeogenic program

step3_graph_traversal.cypher_query:
MATCH path = (src:Drug {name: "Metformin"})-[r*1..3]->(tgt)
WHERE tgt:Protein OR tgt:Pathway OR tgt:Gene
RETURN src, tgt, relationships(path) AS rels
ORDER BY length(path) ASC
LIMIT 50

step6_grounded_generation.final_report_markdown:
# Medinex Research Answer

**Question:** How does Metformin reduce insulin resistance?

## Summary
- Based on the strongest available evidence, metformin reduce insulin resistance is supported by current research. [1] _(confidence: high)_

## Mechanism
- Insulin Resistance impairs Glucose Uptake. [1] _(confidence: high)_
- Metformin inhibits Complex I. [2] _(confidence: high)_
- Metformin activates AMPK. [3] _(confidence: high)_

## Evidence
- Meta-Analysis evidence (JAMA, 2024) supports this relationship. [1] _(confidence: high)_
- Cohort evidence (The Lancet, 2023) supports this relationship. [2] _(confidence: medium)_
- Cohort evidence (Nature Medicine, 2024) supports this relationship. [3] _(confidence: medium)_
- Cohort evidence (Cell, 2023) supports this relationship. [4] _(confidence: medium)_

## Uncertainty
- The strength of the relationship "AMPK inhibits mTOR" is not fully established (confidence: 0.827).  _(confidence: low)_

## References
[1] Metformin and insulin resistance: a systematic clinical review. *JAMA*, 2024. doi:10.1234/diabcare.2024.112 (evidence score: 0.9376)
[2] Mechanisms of metformin action on glucose metabolism and insulin resistance. *The Lancet*, 2023. doi:10.1234/diab.2023.045 (evidence score: 0.6158)
[3] Metformin activates AMP-activated protein kinase in hepatocytes. *Nature Medicine*, 2024. doi:10.1234/jhep.2024.001 (evidence score: 0.6114)
[4] AMPK-mediated phosphorylation of insulin signaling nodes. *Cell*, 2023. doi:10.1234/cellsig.2023.087 (evidence score: 0.45)
